---
author: "Simon Allzén"
title: "Workspace Map"
subtitle: "_Semantic Change / Diachronic Drift of Dark Matter_"

format:
  html:
    theme:
      dark: darkly
      light: flatly
    mainfont: "Fraunces"
    fontsize: "10pt"
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    tabset: true
    minimal: true
    max-width: "1600"
    smooth-scroll: true
    page-layout: article
    df-print: kable
    mermaid:
      theme: neutral

  gfm:
    toc: false
    output-file: repo-map.md
    mermaid-format: svg
---

## Workspace map

This document maps the canonical `semantic-change/workspace` surface. It should be read as the accepted structure map for the workspace.

## At a glance

- `code/notebooks/` contains the accepted notebook spine
- `code/scripts/` contains the accepted Python and R entrypoints
- `code/appendix/` contains the supplementary RDF / triplet branch
- `config/` holds the root sentinel and environment contracts
- `data/` holds standardized paper-local data products
- `local/` holds durable paper-specific assets such as corpora, manual
  curation, and bundled local model assets
- `outputs/` holds derived result families and appendix-facing outputs

## Repository structure

```{text}
workspace/
├── README.md
├── code/
│   ├── notebooks/
│   ├── scripts/
│   └── appendix/
│       ├── README.md
│       ├── APDX_1.0.0-rdf-triplet-extraction-api.ipynb
│       ├── APDX_2.0.1-clean-triplets.ipynb
│       ├── clean_triplets_with_ontology.py
│       ├── APDX_3.0.0-build-triplet-assets.R
│       └── rdf_triplet_extraction.py
├── config/
│   ├── workspace.json
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   └── requirements-R.txt
├── data/
│   ├── parquet/
│   ├── rdata/
│   └── derived/
├── docs/
│   ├── README.qmd
│   └── repo-map.qmd
├── local/
│   ├── corpora/
│   ├── curation/
│   └── models/
└── outputs/
    ├── analytical-results/
    │   ├── analysis_corpus/
    │   ├── lexical_master/
    │   └── models/
    ├── dimension-reduction/
    │   └── models/
    └── appendix/
        ├── figures/
        └── runs/
```

## Structure chart

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 12
%%| fig-height: 7
%%| fig-responsive: false

flowchart LR
    W["workspace/"]
    W --> C["code/"]
    W --> CFG["config/"]
    W --> D["data/"]
    W --> DOC["docs/"]
    W --> L["local/"]
    W --> O["outputs/"]

    C --> NB["notebooks/"]
    C --> S["scripts/"]
    C --> A["appendix/"]

    L --> LC["corpora/"]
    L --> LU["curation/"]
    L --> LM["models/"]

    O --> OA["analytical-results/"]
    O --> OD["dimension-reduction/"]
    O --> OX["appendix/"]
```

## Surface meanings

### code/notebooks/

This is the accepted home of the main notebook spine.

Current accepted notebook surfaces:

- `0.0.0-master-corpus-build.ipynb`
- `0.1.0-corpus-splitting.ipynb`
- `1.0.0-embeddings.ipynb`
- `1.1.0-dimension-reduction.ipynb`
- `2.0.0-seed-classification-gpt.ipynb`
- `2.1.0-train-classification-model.ipynb`
- `2.2.0-run-classification-model.ipynb`
- `3.0.0-empirical-testing.ipynb`
- `3.1.0-significance-testing.ipynb`
- `4.0.0-collostructional-analysis.ipynb`

The main notebook spine is now migrated into the accepted workspace.

### code/scripts/

This is the accepted home of the main Python and R entrypoints.

Current accepted engine/module surface:

- `setup_scibert_model.py`

### code/appendix/

This is the accepted supplementary RDF / triplet branch. It should not be
treated as the primary analytical workflow.

### config/

This directory is foundational.

- `workspace.json` is the only accepted root sentinel
- `requirements.txt` is the canonical Python environment contract
- `requirements-R.txt` is the canonical R environment contract

### data/

This directory is for standardized project-local data products.

- `parquet/` is the intended home of project-local parquet products such as the
  rebuilt ADS spine

At present, `parquet/` is the only active standardized local data surface in
this workspace. Additional subdirectories should be introduced only when they
are genuinely live and documented.

### local/

This directory is for durable project-specific assets that should not be forced
into cross-project standardization.

- `corpora/` for project-local corpus assets
- `curation/` for manual curation resources such as OpenRefine sidecars
- `models/` for bundled project-local model assets such as SciBERT

### outputs/

This directory is for derived outputs rather than authoritative inputs.

- `analytical-results/` is the canonical successor to the older
  `word-emb-context` family
- `dimension-reduction/` is the canonical successor to the older `dim-red`
  family
- `appendix/` is the canonical home of derived appendix-facing outputs

## Root-resolution contract

All executable surfaces in this workspace should resolve paths through:

- `config/workspace.json`
- `../../shared-assets/code/workspace_rooting/workspace_paths.py`
- `../../shared-assets/code/workspace_rooting/workspace_paths.R`

Legacy `_publish.yml` is not part of root detection in the rebuilt
`research/semantic-change` workspace.

## Interpretation guide

- `csv/`, `phrase_corpora/`, `word-emb-context/`, `dim-red/`, `data/local/`,
  `env/`, and the old top-level `notebooks/`, `scripts/`, and `experimental/`
  paths are not canonical in the rebuilt workspace
- `local/` is for durable project-specific assets, not shared assets
- `outputs/` is for derived products, not authoritative inputs
- no `paper/` or `overleaf/` surface is retained in this code-replication bundle
